'Pierres Lake district in Stevens
county will be a big surprise within
the present year" said e. f. Graves,
who returned yesterday from tour
of the mines in that region.


With A, II. Mikeseli, a mining ex-
pert who has recently located here,
but who has had many years ex-
perience in South Africa and in Next
co. Mr.. Graves week ago left to
inspect the silver-lead copper proper
ties all through the Colville valley
and states the conditions he found
astonished him.


with the opening up of the Re-
public railroad the mines of that vi
entity are experiencing life for the
first time" said Mr.. Graves.


Claim after claim is starting up
and the assessment work is about
completed on most of them. The Red
Lion at the base of First Thought
mountain has finished it's annual as
sessment work and is preparing to
forward development. Ore has been
taken out that will run $500 to the
ton.


"A new bridge has been built
across the Kettle river at Rock Cut
postotiice and the road over it will
